questions.q: Who were the parents of Bharata, the emperor after whom the dynasty's land is named?
questions.explanation: Bharata was the son of King Dushyanta and Shakuntala, and the dynasty traced its glory to him.

questions.q: What is the land of the dynasty called in Bharata's honor?
questions.explanation: The land came to be called Bharatavarsha, the realm of Bharata, a name India carries to this day.

questions.q: From whom does the dynasty take the name "Kuru"?
options: A sacred river of the north
King Kuru, an ancestor of the line
The kusha grass of the field
questions.explanation: King Kuru, a descendant of Bharata, gave his name both to the dynasty and to the field of Kurukshetra.

questions.q: Which sacred field did King Kuru plough, sanctifying it for all time?
questions.explanation: Kuru ploughed the field that became Kurukshetra, the plain where his descendants would one day fight.

questions.q: What was the ancestral capital city of the Kuru dynasty?
questions.explanation: Hastinapura, the City of Elephants on the Ganga, was the seat of the Kuru kings.

questions.q: Which son of Shantanu and Ganga survived infancy to continue serving the line?
questions.explanation: Devavrata, the eighth son spared by Ganga, grew up to become Bhishma, the dynasty's great guardian.

questions.q: In the lineage's final split into rival branches, the sons of Pandu were known by what name?
questions.explanation: Pandu's five sons were called the Pandavas, rivals to their cousins the Kauravas.

questions.q: To which great royal line do the Kurus belong?
options: The lunar dynasty descended from the moon
questions.explanation: The Kurus belong to the Chandravamsha, the lunar dynasty descended from Soma, the moon.

questions.q: The hundred Kauravas of the lineage were the sons of which king?
questions.explanation: The blind king Dhritarashtra and Gandhari were the parents of the hundred Kauravas.

questions.q: Which heir, saved in his mother's womb, alone carried the Kuru line beyond the great war?
questions.explanation: Parikshit, son of Abhimanyu and Uttara, was revived by Krishna and became the line's sole surviving thread.

questions.q: Who were Shakuntala's birth parents?
questions.explanation: Shakuntala was born to the sage Vishvamitra and the apsara Menaka, who abandoned her in the forest.

questions.q: Which sage found and raised the abandoned Shakuntala in his hermitage?
questions.explanation: The sage Kanva raised Shakuntala as his own daughter in his forest hermitage.

questions.q: What childhood name did Bharata earn by fearlessly taming wild beasts?
questions.explanation: The boy was called Sarvadamana, the all-tamer, for he played with lions and tigers as a child.

questions.q: Why is Bharata's choice of successor remembered as remarkable?
options: He set aside his own nine sons and installed a worthy heir
He divided the realm equally among nine sons
He left the throne to his only daughter
He returned the crown to his father Dushyanta
questions.explanation: Judging his nine sons unworthy, Bharata adopted Bhumanyu, obtained through Bharadwaja's rites, putting merit above blood.

questions.q: How did the ancestor Puru win the throne from his father Yayati?
options: By taking his father's old age upon himself
questions.explanation: Puru alone agreed to exchange his youth for Yayati's cursed old age, and was rewarded with the kingdom.

questions.q: Who was the father of Yayati in the lunar line?
questions.explanation: Yayati was the son of Nahusha, the king who once briefly held Indra's throne.

questions.q: After whom was the capital Hastinapura named?
options: A hill shaped like an elephant
The elephant corps of King Kuru
questions.explanation: King Hastin, an early ancestor of the line, founded the city that bore his name, the City of Elephants.

questions.q: Who was the father of King Shantanu?
questions.explanation: Shantanu was born to King Pratipa in the king's old age, after long austerities.

questions.q: Which elder brother of Shantanu renounced the world instead of taking the throne?
questions.explanation: Devapi, the eldest, became an ascetic, leaving the succession to pass toward Shantanu.

questions.q: Chitrangada and Vichitravirya, the short-lived heirs, were the sons of which couple?
questions.explanation: They were the two sons born to Shantanu and his second wife, the fisher queen Satyavati.

questions.q: How did the young king Chitrangada meet his death?
options: At his own brother's hand in a quarrel
From wounds taken on a boar hunt
Slain by a gandharva who bore the same name
questions.explanation: A gandharva also named Chitrangada challenged the proud young king and killed him in a long duel.

questions.q: When Vichitravirya died childless, who fathered Dhritarashtra, Pandu, and Vidura to continue the lineage?
questions.explanation: By the custom of niyoga, the sage Vyasa fathered the three princes upon the widowed queens and a maid.

questions.q: The lunar line descends from the moon through which ancestor, famous for his love of the apsara Urvashi?
questions.explanation: Pururavas, grandson of the moon through Budha and Ila, loved Urvashi and fathered the line's continuation.

questions.q: Who was the mother of Puru, the ancestor from whom the dynasty descends?
questions.explanation: Puru was Yayati's son by Sharmishtha, the asura princess, not by his first wife Devayani.

questions.q: In Vyasa's Mahabharata, what finally made Dushyanta acknowledge Shakuntala and her son?
options: Dushyanta found his lost signet ring
A voice from the heavens confirmed her word
The sage Kanva arrived bearing proof
The boy strung the royal bow
questions.explanation: In the epic a celestial voice vouched for Shakuntala; the famous lost-ring device belongs to Kalidasa's later play.

questions.q: What boon was attached to the field that King Kuru ploughed?
options: No weapon could ever be forged there
Kings would always be crowned there
Those who die there attain heaven
questions.explanation: Kuru's austerities won the promise that those who die at Kurukshetra rise to heaven, sanctifying the future battlefield.

questions.q: Which brother of Shantanu left Hastinapura to rule in his maternal uncle's distant kingdom?
questions.explanation: Bahlika settled in the northwestern realm of Bahli, leaving the Hastinapura succession to Shantanu.

questions.q: Why was Pandu crowned king although Dhritarashtra was the elder brother?
options: He won a contest of arms
Dhritarashtra's blindness barred him from the throne
questions.explanation: Law held that a blind man could not rule, so the crown passed over Dhritarashtra to Pandu.

questions.q: After Parikshit, which king continued the Kuru line and heard the epic recited?
questions.explanation: Parikshit's son Janamejaya succeeded him, and at his snake sacrifice the Mahabharata was first recited.

questions.q: Strictly speaking, to whom does the title "Kaurava", descendant of Kuru, apply?
options: Only the hundred sons of Dhritarashtra
Only the five sons of Pandu
Both the sons of Dhritarashtra and the sons of Pandu
questions.explanation: Both rival branches descend from King Kuru, though usage reserves the name Kaurava for Dhritarashtra's sons.
